title: Analyst, Data Integrity
description:
**THE AGENCY**



OMG23 is the Omnicom Media Group agency dedicated to Walt Disney Studios, Disney\\+ and Disney's multiple television networks. At OMG23, we have the privilege of partnering with Disney on the planning, buying and implementation of its integrated ad campaigns for some of the entertainment industry's most iconic brands. Our Walt Disney Studios team works on titles that include a wide variety of blockbuster films distributed under the Walt Disney Studios, Marvel Studios, Lucasfilm, Pixar Animation Studios, Walt Disney Animation Studios, 20th Century, and Searchlight banners. Our Disney Entertainment TV teams work on tune in and streaming for networks including ABC, ABC News, Disney Channels, Freeform, National Geographic and FX. We live movies and TV. If you do too, see below for an opportunity to join our team.

**The Job:**



OMG23 \\| Disney Entertainment \\| Data and Insights



OMG23 is looking for a data\\-driven Analyst to join our Marketing Science team. In this role you'll turn complex data into insights that shape how audiences experience the world's most iconic entertainment brands. You'll track performance, uncover trends, and partner with internal teams and vendors to translate numbers into action.



If you love data, storytelling, and the intersection of marketing and analytics, this is your next move.


**What You'll Do:**


* Analyze and interpret campaign data to deliver clear, actionable insights
* Build and maintain reports through analytics and measurement platforms
* Track KPIs, performance benchmarks, and campaign outcomes across media channels
* Present findings and recommendations to internal teams and clients
* Identify opportunities to streamline reporting and improve analytics workflows
* Mentor and support on data processes and best practices


**You Bring:**


* Bachelor's degree in statistics, mathematics, economics, computer science, data science, marketing, or a related field; MS/MBA a plus
* 1\\+ years of experience in a quantitative or data analytics role; advertising agency experience preferred
* Strong analytical mindset with attention to detail and storytelling skills
* Proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint, familiarity with SQL and database concepts
* Hands\\-on experience with analytics tools such as Google Analytics, Campaign Manager, Innovid, or Meta Ads Manager
* Exposure to SAS, SPSS, R, or similar statistical software
* Strong communicator (written and verbal)
* Flexible and adaptable in fast\\-moving environments
* Proactive problem\\-solver with sharp critical thinking
* Breaks down complex ideas into simple, actionable takeaways


Join a team that blends creativity, data, and innovation to power the future of entertainment marketing. You'll gain hands\\-on experience, mentorship, and the chance to shape how data drives impact across media.


***T******h******is is a hybrid role based in Burbank, CA.***



\\#LI\\-MC1



*This role is hybrid, requiring three (3\\) days per week in the office. The remaining two (2\\) days may be worked remotely. Specific in\\-office days will be discussed during the interview process, with flexibility to align with team needs. Please note that the number or required in\\-office days may be adjusted over time, potentially increasing the number of required in\\-office days based on business needs.*

  


***Review Our Recruitment Privacy Notice***

title: Senior Digital Analyst
description:
**About Ruggable:**



Ruggable is a leading direct\\-to\\-consumer e\\-commerce brand based in Los Angeles, California with an extraordinary track record of high, profitable growth. We pride ourselves on having an extremely loyal customer base and a talented team made up of genuinely caring people who take action and deliver results. We are venture\\-backed and own a patented washable rug design that's disrupting the home décor industry. Our mission is to empower our customers to live vibrantly with beautiful products that don't compromise on function. If you're passionate about consumer products, e\\-commerce, and high\\-growth start\\-ups, keep reading!

**Job Summary:**



We are seeking a highly analytical, systems\\-oriented, and seasoned Senior Digital Analyst to lead the reporting integrity and performance visibility for the Ruggable site. Reporting to the Director of Reporting, Analytics \\& Insights, you will serve as primary authority on site data, ensuring our data architecture, from reporting to user journey analyses, is accurate, actionable and scalable.



The Senior Digital Analyst translates raw data into commercial action for the site. You are a power user of analytics platforms such as GA4 and Heap, with the technical agility to translate raw events data into user journeys and partner with Data Engineering to build reporting for insights. Beyond reporting, you are a strategic partner in site experimentation and personalization, with experience in platforms such as Ninetailed, Optimizely and Constructor, and providing clear performance/statistical results as well as scalable recommendations.


**What You'll Do:**


#### **Core eCommerce \\& Site Performance**


* Site Real Estate: Analyze and report on the performance of PLPs, PDPs, and core content pages to ensure optimal user flow and product discovery.
* Device \\& Funnel Analysis: Monitor and diagnose variances in the conversion funnel across Mobile vs. Desktop, identifying UX opportunities or technical friction unique to each device.
* Traffic Attribution: Analyze collection launch and site performance by traffic channel, ensuring a clear understanding of how different segments interact with site features.
* Commercial Metrics: Own the reporting for Discount vs. Non\\-Discount sales, Promo vs. Non\\-Promo lift, and the performance of New vs. Repeat customers over time.

#### **Revenue Forecasting \\& Strategic Tracking**


* Dynamic Forecasting: Support the continuous forecasting and re\\-forecasting of site KPIs, breaking down targets by traffic channel and customer segment (New vs. Repeat).
* Performance Accountability: Track real\\-time performance against forecasts and historical benchmarks, providing proactive "flash reports" on AOV, conversion, and RPV when the business is over/under\\-indexing.
* Market Context: Partner with external vendors to track market share and competitor activity, translating external market shifts into context for our internal performance.

#### **Customer Insights \\& Cross\\-Functional Strategy**


* 1st \\& 3rd Party Data Integration: Lead the exploration of customer data by stitching together 1st\\-party data (geo, cohort, products purchased) with 3rd\\-party datasets to build a 360\\-degree view of the Ruggable shopper.
* Consumer Snapshots: Own the end\\-to\\-end refresh of the Quarterly Consumer Snapshot deck, providing the definitive narrative on customer demographics and shifting purchase behaviors.
* The Cross\\-Functional Bridge: Serve as the primary analytical liaison for the Commercial team, ensuring customer insights are not just "reported," but are consistently applied to product launches, marketing creative, and site strategy.

#### **Testing \\& Personalization (CRO)**


* Experimentation: Lead the analysis for A/B testing and personalization initiatives using Optimizely and Ninetailed, ensuring statistical significance and internal validation of all test outcomes and incremental lift.
* Actionable Readouts: Translate complex test results into clear, "so\\-what" narratives that provide the Digital team with scalable recommendations for optimization.


**Analytical Operations \\& Strategic Governance**


* BI Engineering (Sigma): Lead the buildout and validation of automated eCommerce dashboards in Sigma, translating complex business logic into intuitive, self\\-service reporting for stakeholders.
* Data Stewardship \\& Integrity: Partner with Data Engineering to optimize Snowflake tables and implement rigorous validation/alerting systems, ensuring our data infrastructure remains the "absolute source of truth."
* Performance Storytelling: Own the end\\-to\\-end delivery of weekly and monthly business reviews, providing context\\-rich narratives that diagnose the impact of site merchandising and product launches.
* Technical Liaison: Bridge the gap between Tech and Digital leadership by translating commercial needs into technical specifications (e.g., event tracking requirements).
* Advanced Ad\\-Hoc Synthesis: Rapidly respond to high\\-stakes requests from leadership by "stitching" together data from disparate sources—Shopify, Heap, Optimizely, Ninetailed, and Constructor—to provide decision\\-ready insights.


**What You'll Need to Have:**


**Required:**


* Experience: 4–5 years of professional experience with a proven track record in Ecom and Revenue analytics (ideally in a high\\-growth DTC environment).
* Communication \\& Synthesis: A strong, persuasive communicator with the ability to distill complex, multi\\-variate analyses into simple, digestible, and actionable insights for non\\-technical stakeholders.
* Platform Mastery: Deep, hands\\-on experience in GA4, Heap, and Shopify; must be a power user of Excel for advanced modeling and data stitching.
* Technical Agility: Advanced SQL skills and a proven ability to quickly learn and master new platform UIs, or BI tools (Sigma).
* BI Expertise: Direct experience with Sigma and/or Looker (LookML). Experience navigating BI migrations is a major plus.
* Metric Fluency: Expert\\-level understanding of the e\\-commerce funnel, customer behavior metrics, and the interplay between promotional elasticity and site performance.
* Collaborative Bridge: Proven experience acting as a technical liaison between Data Engineering/Tech teams and Ecom stakeholders to drive high\\-impact projects.
* Growth Mindset: A self\\-starter who thrives in a fast\\-paced, high\\-ownership environment and is excited by the prospect of building scalable processes from the ground up.


**Preferred:**


* Data Engineering Exposure: Experience working within Snowflake or AWS environments and a solid understanding of data warehousing and governance.
* Coding Exposure: Experience with R or Python for data manipulation.
* Engineering Workflow: Familiarity with version control (e.g., GitHub) and working within an agile framework alongside Data Engineering teams.


**Compensation:**


* Annual base salary between $120,000 \\- $140,000
* An annual bonus percentage that varies based on level of role.
* Employer matching (up to 3% of base salary) for company sponsored 401K plan.


At Ruggable, we offer competitive compensation and benefits packages. Ruggable is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We proudly recruit and hire a diverse workforce and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.


*If you are based in California, we encourage you to read this important information for California residents* linked here.


**To all recruitment agencies:** Ruggable does not accept unsolicited agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Ruggable employees or any other company destination. Ruggable is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

title: Reporting and Data Analyst
description:
At IDS – Integrated Default Solutions, we provide technology solutions that support the mortgage servicing and default management industries. We offer software development, web\\-based solutions consulting, and network infrastructure support to enhance efficiency and compliance for servicers, investors, and financial institutions.


Headquartered in San Diego, with team members across multiple states, we offer opportunities for both on\\-site and remote work. At IDS, we prioritize collaboration, problem\\-solving, and continuous improvement, ensuring a supportive environment for professional growth.


Employees enjoy competitive compensation, performance\\-based incentives, and a comprehensive benefits package designed to support well\\-being and career development.

**Summary:** **We are seeking a mid\\-level Power BI \\& SQL Data Analyst to support and enhance our business intelligence and reporting capabilities. This role is ideal for a professional with strong hands\\-on experience in Power BI and SQL who can independently deliver production\\-ready reports and dashboards while collaborating with business stakeholders and senior technical staff.**
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

* Develop and maintain Power BI reports and dashboards used by operational teams and leadership
* Write, optimize, and maintain T\\-SQL queries, views, and stored procedures in Microsoft SQL Server and MS Analysis Services.
* Support and enhance existing data models used for reporting and analytics
* Assist with ETL processes (SSIS or similar tools) to load and transform data from source systems
* Work with business stakeholders to gather reporting requirements and translate them into Power BI solutions
* Validate data accuracy and troubleshoot reporting, data quality, or performance issues
* Document reports, datasets, and data flows to support ongoing maintenance and knowledge sharing
* Collaborate with senior team members on larger BI and data initiatives

**Required Qualifications (Strong Mid\\-Level)**
-----------------------------------------------

* 4–5 years of experience in a Power BI, Business Intelligence, or SQL\\-focused data role
* Strong hands\\-on experience with Microsoft Power BI, including data modeling, Power Query, and intermediate DAX
* Solid proficiency in Microsoft SQL Server (T\\-SQL), including complex queries, stored procedures, and basic performance tuning
* Experience supporting production BI reports and dashboards used by business stakeholders or leadership
* Working knowledge of data warehousing concepts and reporting best practices
* Ability to independently gather reporting requirements and translate them into effective, user\\-friendly Power BI solutions
* Comfortable troubleshooting data quality, performance, and reporting issues in an existing environment
* Strong communication skills with the ability to explain data insights to non\\-technical users

**Preferred / Nice\\-to\\-Have Skills**
-------------------------------------

* Experience with SSIS or other ETL and data integration tools
* Familiarity with performance tuning and working with large datasets
* Background in professional services, legal, finance, or regulated industries

**Work Schedule:**


The work week is typically Monday through Friday, with business hours from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. After an initial 90\\-day training period and upon management approval, there will be some flexibility for remote work.

**Salary Range:**


The salary for this position typically ranges from $24\\.00\\-30\\.00 per hour, depending on qualifications, experience, and other factors. Please note that the final offer may differ based on the candidate's specific qualifications, skills, and experience, as well as internal equity and business needs.

**Benefits:**


Integrated Default Solutions and affiliated companies promote work/life balance with a robust wellness program, PTO, remote work, and flexible schedules (where available). Full\\-time employees become eligible for benefits following a 30\\-day waiting period, with benefit offerings that include medical, dental, vision, life, AD\\&D, EAP, STD, and LTD. Additionally the firm provides parental leave for both primary and non\\-primary caregivers as well. Also available are voluntary income protection benefits such as supplemental life, accident, critical illness, and short and long\\-term care insurances, as well as a 401(k)\\-retirement plan with a company match. Part\\-time employees may have access to some of these benefits, which may be on a pro\\-rated basis.

**Security Requirement:**


While performing the duties of this job, the employee is required to ensure the security and confidentiality of all sensitive information, including but not limited to threats or hazards to the security or integrity of sensitive information that could result in any harm or inconvenience to any customer, employee or company.

**Work Environment:**


A corporate office environment with a professional setting, characterized by a quiet to moderate noise level. Employees may work in individual or shared workspaces, with standard office equipment such as computers, printers, and telephones. Occasional meetings, collaborative discussions, and business activities may contribute to variations in noise levels.

**Notices:**


The above information on this job description has been described to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by incumbents. Other duties and responsibilities not specifically described may be assigned from time to time, consistent with knowledge, skills and abilities of the incumbent. Integrated Default Solutions and our affiliate companies are Equal Opportunity Employers. We are committed to providing a work environment free from discrimination and harassment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We celebrate diversity and are dedicated to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Integrated Default Solutions and our affiliate companies will consider qualified applicants with a criminal history pursuant to the San Diego County Fair Chance Ordinance and the California Fair Chance Act. You do not need to disclose your criminal history or participate in a background check until a conditional job offer is made to you. After making a conditional offer and running a background check, if Integrated Default Solutions and our affiliate companies are concerned about conviction that is directly related to the job, you will be given the chance to explain the circumstances surrounding the conviction, provide mitigating evidence, or challenge the accuracy of the background report. Find out more about the Fair Chance Ordinance by visiting the San Diego County Office of Labor Stands and Enforcement webpage. As part of our commitment to maintaining a lawful and compliant workforce, Integrated Default Solutions and our affiliate companies participate in the E\\-Verify program. All candidates who accept a job offer will be required to complete the E\\-Verify process to verify their employment eligibility in the United States.


Applications will be accepted until 03/31/2026\\.

title: Data & Content Management
description:
**Job Description:**

**Title:** Retailer Data Analyst


**Location:** Remote – USA, Canada OR Mexico


**Reports to:** Director of Data and Content Management, OEM Solutions – Customer Solutions


**Vacancy** : This is an existing vacancy  

  



**The Role:**

The Retail Analyst is a key member of the Data Quality and Insights team responsible for validating and ensuring the accuracy of automotive retail dealership data within the Power Information Network (PIN). This data serves as the foundation for critical products, analytics, and services delivered to automotive manufacturers, retail dealerships, financial institutions, government agencies, and authorized data partners. The analyst plays a vital role in maintaining data integrity, supporting operational excellence, and enhancing the value and reliability of PIN’s data assets.  



  

**The Impact You Will Have in This Role:**

The data reviews and related functions serve as the foundation for critical products, analytics, and services delivered to automotive manufacturers, retail dealerships, financial institutions, government agencies, and authorized data partners. The analyst plays a vital role in maintaining data integrity, supporting operational excellence, and enhancing the value and reliability of PIN’s data assets.


**What You’ll Be Doing in This Role:**

* Analysis of dealership transactional data
* Calculate and verify financial information using transmitted data elements.
* Look for data element population trends
* Compile list of concerns regarding questionable data.
* Periodically engage with dealership accounting personnel to confirm that our results match theirs.
* Use of Microsoft Excel analytical tools, SQL queries, AIC queries, custom query tools, and SalesForce CRM.
* Logging of dealership “status”.
* Addition of summaries to PIN retailer databases.
* Perform quality checks on samples from retailer reports.
* As received from Customer Care team, perform data analysis investigations.
* Coordinate with Customer Care team to communicate investigation results to retailer clients.
* Vendor data analysis

**Qualifications of this Role:** 

3\\+ years of experience in one or more of the following areas:


* Automotive Sales Accounting
* Automotive Finance \\& Insurance (F\\&I)
* Automotive Data or Analytics

Associate degree (AA) or higher, or equivalent professional experience


Demonstrated expertise in automotive accounting, F\\&I operations, or automotive data analysis


Ability to work remotely while effectively collaborating with a team to resolve data‑related issues


Strong analytical, organizational, and problem‑solving skills


Excellent communication, interpersonal, and customer service abilities for working with both technical and functional users


Proficiency with Excel, SQL, and Microsoft Office Suite


**The Hiring Manager says:**

We’re looking for someone who is naturally curious and loves working with data. The ideal candidate thrives on solving complex problems, collaborating with dealership personnel, and tackling new challenges with a positive attitude. They enjoy being part of a supportive team but are equally comfortable operating independently and taking ownership of their work.


This position has a starting hourly rate of $28\\. This is the rate we reasonably and in good faith expect to pay for the role at the time of posting. An employee’s pay within the range is determined by a number of factors, including relevant skills, education, qualifications, experience, performance, business or organizational needs, and geographic location.


**Company Mission**

J.D. Power is clear about what we do to ensure our success into the future. We unite industry leading data and insights with world\\-class technology to solve our clients’ toughest challenges.


**Our Values**

We POWER Our Customer's Success


We are Innovative, Collaborative and Grounded in Data


We Make Things Easy


We Get It Done


We Start with Trust \\& Prove it Everyday


J.D. Power is committed to employing a diverse workforce. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability.


Should you require accommodations during the recruitment and selection process, please reach out to tarecruitment@jdpa.com.

Power has international sites and J.D. Power uses resources located throughout the world. J.D. Power may from time to time also use third parties to act on J.D. Power’s behalf. title: Staff Business Data Analyst Expert Network
description:
**Overview**
------------


Join the Intuit Customer Success team as a **Staff Business Data Analyst** focused on our **Expert Network**. In this role, you will define the analytics strategy to optimize our live experiences and professional network. You will be a central figure in driving large\\-scale data migration and strategy, ensuring our data infrastructure is robust, scalable, and transitions seamlessly from legacy systems to a future\\-ready target state.


As a **Staff BDA**, you will lead complex projects that translate business challenges into analytical frameworks. You will partner closely with Data Engineering, Product Management, and Strategy teams to build the data architecture and measurement methodologies that inform high\\-stakes business decisions and drive our AI\\-driven platform forward.

 **Responsibilities**

* **Strategic Migration \\& Mapping:** Lead data architecture and instrumentation initiatives to map complex data from legacy systems into standardized, high\\-quality data products
* **Analytical Strategy:** Turn business strategy into defined analytical questions and sound hypotheses; develop KPI frameworks that set the direction for the Expert Network.
* **Data Modeling \\& Lifecycle:** Develop optimal, scalable data solutions and models with a "scale\\-first" mindset, ensuring rigorous data stewardship and compliance

title: Assistant Sustainability Analyst
description:
The City of Thousand Oaks Public Works Department is seeking an Assistant Analyst to support the Sustainability Division in advancing the City’s sustainability and energy initiatives. This position plays a key role in managing and analyzing complex data related to energy, water, waste, and electric vehicle infrastructure, while supporting implementation of the Climate and Environmental Action Plan (CEAP).

The Assistant Analyst assists with benchmarking, reporting, GIS mapping, and coordination of external energy programs, as well as contract development and tracking funding opportunities to ensure compliance and maximize program impact. Through public outreach, vendor coordination, and continuous improvement efforts, this role helps advance innovative solutions that promote energy efficiency, renewable energy adoption, and the City’s long\\-term sustainability and environmental goals.

**SALARY** 

The anticipated hiring range for the Assistant Analyst position is **$80,594 \\- $100,743** annually, with a full salary range up to $120,892\\. The City typically makes offers within the hiring range based on the candidate’s experience and qualifications.

**PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES:**

* Support in collecting, cleaning, and analyzing sustainability data, including energy, water, waste, and EV charging
* Assist with the development of dashboards, reports, and data visualizations for internal and public use
* Support sustainability benchmarking, ongoing reporting, and greenhouse gas (GHG) inventory updates
* Assist with CEAP implementation through data updates, progress tracking, KPI reporting, and documentation
* Provide GIS mapping support for EV charging, solar installations, energy use intensity, and retrofit prioritization
* Coordinate the City’s participation in regional and utility energy programs (e.g., SoCalREN, C3 REN, SCE), including scheduling, reporting, incentive tracking, and partner meetings
* Assist with contract development and administration for EV charging, energy efficiency, solar, and battery storage projects, including procurement support and performance tracking
* Research and report on grants, incentives, rebates, and regulatory changes related to energy and transportation electrification
* Monitor EV charger performance and coordinate with vendors on maintenance, outages, and software issues
* Support public outreach efforts, including workshops, educational materials, and website updates
* Coordinate with contractors, utilities, regional partners, and consultants to track deliverables and resolve issues
* Assist with grant application development, including data collection, narrative drafting, and deadline tracking
* Track emerging technologies, best practices, and tools that support municipal sustainability goals

**THE IDEAL CANDIDATE SHOULD POSSESS:**

* Knowledge of principles and practices related to sustainability, energy management, climate action planning, and environmental data analysis within a municipal or public\\-sector context
* Experience in quantitative data collection, cleaning, analyzing, and interpreting quantitative data related to energy, water, solid waste, greenhouse gas emissions, and electric vehicle charging infrastructure
* Knowledge of sustainability\\-related programs and regulations, including solid waste (e.g., SB 1383\\), building codes, and electric vehicle charging infrastructure requirements. Familiarity with GHG inventory methodologies and KPI tracking for climate or environmental action plans.
* Ability to research and track grants, incentives, rebates, and regulatory changes related to energy and transportation electrification
* Excellent verbal and written communication/interpersonal skills
* Ability to prepare written materials and outreach content and support public education and engagement efforts
* Ability to communicate effectively with City staff, contractors, utilities, partner agencies, and the public
* Strong organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities, meet deadlines, and maintain accurate records
* Ability to work independently and collaboratively, exercise sound judgment, and adapt to changing priorities
* Professional, tactful, and collaborative approach with the ability to build effective working relationships
* A valid Class C California driver’s license is required

**ADDITIONAL DESIRABLE QUALIFICATIONS INCLUDE:**

* Experience supporting benchmarking, performance monitoring, and development of reports, dashboards, and data visualizations
* Experience in contract development, procurement, and project administration, including performance and compliance tracking

**EDUCATION**  
A Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university with major course work in environmental or physical science, sustainability, resource management, urban planning, math, engineering, energy, solid waste, climate science, or a similar field.

**EXPERIENCE**  
Two years of professional experience in energy conservation and/or related sustainability areas (e.g., water efficiency, energy management, or climate action), including a minimum of one year in research, administrative support, or analytical work. Experience in public administration is preferred.

Job Type: Full\\-time

Pay: $80,594\\.00 \\- $120,892\\.00 per year

* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance

title: Security Analyst
description:
Vallarta Supermarkets is a proud family\\-founded grocery chain rooted in authentic Mexican heritage and vibrant Latin culture. What began as a single neighborhood market has grown into one of the fastest\\-growing supermarket chains in California, bringing fresh foods, traditional flavors, and warm hospitality to the communities we serve. As we continue our rapid expansion, we remain committed to celebrating culture, creating opportunity, and treating every customer and team member like family.


Vallarta Supermarkets is seeking a proactive and detail\\-oriented IT Security Analyst to join our Technology and Security team. This role is responsible for monitoring, analyzing, and responding to security threats across our enterprise IT infrastructure, including store systems, corporate networks, cloud environments, and retail platforms.


The ideal candidate will bring strong technical expertise, sound judgment under pressure, and a passion for protecting systems, sensitive data, and users from evolving cyber risks. This role plays a critical part in safeguarding business continuity across our retail operations.


Base Salary: $90,000 – $120,000/year


**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

* Monitor security alerts, logs, and events across SIEM and related security tools.
* Investigate potential security incidents and respond in accordance with established incident response procedures.
* Conduct root cause analysis and document findings to strengthen preventive controls.
* Assist in proactive threat hunting activities to identify vulnerabilities and suspicious behaviors.
* Perform vulnerability assessments and coordinate remediation efforts with infrastructure and application teams.
* Support audits and compliance initiatives (e.g., ISO 27001, NIST, SOC 2, PCI\\-DSS).
* Maintain and enhance security policies, standards, and procedures.
* Manage and optimize security technologies such as firewalls, endpoint protection platforms, and email security gateways.
* Support Identity and Access Management (IAM), including user provisioning, deprovisioning, and periodic access reviews.
* Assist with patch management and system hardening initiatives across corporate and store environments.
* Participate in security awareness training and phishing simulation campaigns.


Provide guidance to employees on secure technology usage and cybersecurity best practices

* 2–4\\+ years of experience in IT security, cybersecurity operations, or a related technical role.
* Strong understanding of networking concepts, operating systems, and common attack vectors.
* Hands\\-on experience with SIEM platforms (e.g., Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel), EDR tools, and vulnerability scanners.
* Ability to analyze data from multiple sources and respond effectively to security events.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills with strong documentation abilities.

**Preferred Qualifications**
----------------------------

* Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity, Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field.
* Industry certifications such as Security\\+, CEH, CySA\\+, GSEC, or equivalent.
* Experience with cloud security in Azure, AWS, or GCP environments.
* Knowledge of scripting languages (e.g., Python, PowerShell) for automation.
* Familiarity with Zero Trust principles, IAM frameworks, and secure configuration standards.
* Experience supporting PCI\\-DSS compliance within a retail environment.

**Key Competencies**
--------------------

* Strong analytical and investigative mindset.
* High attention to detail and accuracy.
* Ability to remain calm and decisive during high\\-pressure incidents.
* Strong cross\\-functional collaboration skills.
* Continuous learning mindset with a proactive approach to emerging threats.

**Communication \\& Interpersonal Effectiveness**
------------------------------------------------


The Cybersecurity Security Analyst must effectively communicate complex security concepts, risks, and mitigation strategies to diverse populations, including corporate leadership, retail operations teams, store personnel, IT staff, third\\-party vendors, and external partners.


This includes the ability to:


Translate technical cybersecurity findings into clear, actionable business language

* Communicate security risks, incidents, and remediation steps to non\\-technical audiences
* Prepare written reports, incident documentation, and executive summaries
* Deliver presentations, training sessions, and awareness briefings (in person and virtual)
* Collaborate cross\\-functionally with Loss Prevention, Facilities, HR, Legal, Operations, and executive leadership
* Engage respectfully and professionally with individuals from diverse cultural, educational, and professional backgrounds


The successful candidate will demonstrate cultural competency, discretion, professionalism, and the ability to communicate effectively during high\\-pressure or incident response situations.

**Physical Demands \\& Work Environment (California Compliant)**
---------------------------------------------------------------


Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions of this position, in accordance with applicable California and federal law.


While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to:

* Sit, stand, and walk for extended periods of time
* Move between corporate offices, retail locations, data centers, and security areas
* Bend, kneel, crouch, stoop, and reach to access network closets, POS systems, IDF/MDF rooms, security hardware, and under\\-desk or ceiling\\-mounted equipment
* Climb stairs and occasionally use step stools or ladders to access equipment
* Use hands and fingers to handle, install, configure, and secure technical devices and cabling
* Lift and/or move equipment weighing up to **40 pounds** unassisted and up to **75 pounds** with assistance
* Carry laptops, diagnostic equipment, security appliances, monitors, and related peripherals between locations

### **Work Environment Conditions**


The role may involve working in:

* Corporate office settings
* Retail store floors during operational hours
* Stockrooms, back\\-of\\-house areas, and equipment closets
* Data centers or telecommunications rooms


Work conditions may include:

* Exposure to cords, cables, and equipment creating potential **trip, slip, and fall hazards**
* Limited\\-space or confined technical areas
* Moderate noise levels in retail or server environments
* Occasional after\\-hours or emergency response work during cybersecurity incidents
* Travel between retail locations within the region
* Employees are expected to comply with all workplace safety, information security, and corporate policies and procedures.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
**About Crunchyroll**

Founded by fans, Crunchyroll delivers the art and culture of anime to a passionate community. We super\\-serve over 100 million anime and manga fans across 200\\+ countries and territories, and help them connect with the stories and characters they crave. The Center for Data and Insights, Global Business Analytics team is looking for an experienced Senior Data Analyst with a passion for understanding data, creating engaging visualizations, tracking business trends and storytelling. title: Sr Product Analyst II
description:
**GROW WITH US:**  

Tandem Diabetes Care creates new possibilities for people living with diabetes, their loved ones, and their healthcare providers through a positively different experience. We’d love for you to team up with us to “innovate every day,” put “people first,” and take the “no\\-shortcuts” approach that has propelled us to become a leader in the diabetes technology industry.

 **STAY AWESOME:**  

Tandem Diabetes Care is proud to manufacture and sell the Tandem Mobi system and t:slim X2 insulin pump with Control\\-IQ\\+ technology — an advanced predictive algorithm that automates insulin delivery. But we’re so much more than that. Our company’s human\\-centered approach to design, development, and support delivers innovative products and services for people who use insulin. Because many of our own team members live with diabetes, or have a loved one impacted by diabetes, the work is personal, and we are committed to the cause. Learn more at tandemdiabetes.com

**A DAY IN THE LIFE:**


The Senior Product Analyst II serves as a strategic partner to product, engineering, and business stakeholders, driving data\\-informed decision making across the product lifecycle by translating complex datasets into actionable insights, scalable analytics solutions, and clear narratives. With strong technical expertise, product acumen, customer empathy, and cross\\-functional influence, this role advances business objectives while leading the development and governance of product metrics, dashboards, and self\\-service analytics capabilities, partnering across teams to improve product performance, user experience, and operational efficiency through rigorous analysis and data\\-driven recommendations.
A Senior Product Analyst II at Tandem also:

* Works within an agile scrum framework to support product development and iterative delivery.
* Partners with product, engineering, UX, and business teams to define success metrics, measurement plans, and analytic approaches.
* Translates product and business questions into structured analytical frameworks and clear, actionable recommendations.
* Creates intuitive dashboards, KPI views, and reporting systems using Power BI.
* Leverages modern analytics techniques, including Python, SQL, and AI/ML tools, to automate repeatable analyses and accelerate insight generation.
* Conducts deep\\-dive analyses on adoption, system performance, and user experience to uncover actionable insights.
* Collaborates with systems engineering to define requirements for data models, pipelines, event tracking, and system integrations.
* Partners with product management and systems engineering to understand how product components operate, interact, and influence downstream data.
* Translates business concepts into clean, maintainable data structures optimized for business intelligence and self\\-service analytics.
* Evaluates and recommends emerging analytics technologies to improve productivity and insight quality.
* Enables teams to self\\-serve by training stakeholders on metric definitions, dashboard usage, and data interpretation.
* Drives a culture of evidence\\-based decision making across product and operational teams.
* Ensures analytical data quality through validation, quality assurance processes, and automated monitoring where applicable.
* Ensures compliance with company policies, including Privacy/HIPAA, and other legal and regulatory requirements.
* Other responsibilities as assigned.

This position will require you to come into the Del Mar Headquarters as needed based on business demands. Tandem will provide equipment for you to work remotely when not in office.

**WHAT YOU’LL NEED:**

* B.S. in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science, Statistics, Physics, etc.
* 8\\+ years experience working as a data analyst, or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
* Strong analytical and problem\\-solving skills.
* Advanced proficiency in SQL and at least one analytical programming language (Python or R).
* Expertise with modern BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar)
* Strong understanding of product analytics concepts: metrics frameworks, funnel analysis, segmentation, experimentation design, etc.
* Ability to translate data insights into compelling narratives for executives and cross‑functional partners.
* Experience working with large data sets and modern data platforms (databricks, snowflake)
* Experience working within agile teams.

The starting base pay range for this position is **$130,000k\\-$160,000** annually. Base pay will vary based on job\\-related knowledge, skills, experience and may also fluctuate depending on candidate’s location and the overall job market. In addition to base pay, Tandem offers a competitive compensation package that includes bonus and a robust benefits package.


Tandem offers health care benefits such as medical, dental, vision available your first day, as well as health savings accounts and flexible saving accounts. You’ll also receive 11 paid holidays per year, a minimum of 20 days of paid time off (with accrual starting on day 1\\) and you will have access to a 401k plan with company match as well as an Employee Stock Purchase plan. Learn more about Tandem’s benefits here!

**YOU SHOULD KNOW:**

Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with applicable state and local Fair Chance laws and regulations. This review is required to ensure that individuals in such roles uphold high standards of trust and integrity so as to protect the interests of our customers, employees, and stakeholders.

**SPONSORSHIP:** Applicants must be authorized to work for any employer in the U.S. We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time.

**WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE:**  

At Tandem, we believe joy fuels excellence. That's why we've built a workplace that celebrates your achievements and supports your well\\-being. Our team thrives on pushing boundaries and fostering growth, all while maintaining a spirit of fun and camaraderie. This is just one of the ways we stay awesome! Explore the benefits and reasons to love Tandem at https://www.tandemdiabetes.com/careers.

description:

description:

title: Supervisor Clinical Intelligence

Across all of this, we are united by a shared commitment: combining compassion with discipline, innovation with stewardship, and growth with integrity.

Additionally, you will manage and oversee development and production of regulatory network filing artifacts as required by regulators for all BSC and Promise Health Plan Commercial, Medi\\-Cal, and Medicare networks. Our leadership model is about developing great leaders at all levels and creating opportunities for our people to grow – personally, professionally, and financially. We are looking for leaders that are energized by creative and critical thinking, building and sustaining high\\-performing teams, getting results the right way, and fostering continuous learning.


  
**Your Work**  

In this role, you will:


* Develop and conduct evaluations and studies within limited time frames and produce analyses that are easy for non\\-technical persons to understand
* Interact with internal and external customers to define complex reporting needs and specifications; design and implement complex report programming according to specifications
* Present effectively to internal and external customers on analytics/reports/presentations that are critical for their business continuity and decision making
* Participate in internal and external task forces and committees to provide analytical insights and support for implementation of new programs, applications, products and services and enhancement of existing applications/systems
* Independently summarize findings and present data in formats such as Excel pivots, PowerPoint, reports, grids and tables
* Lead and manage projects that may involve coordinating a team and working with different stakeholders
* Manage creation, collection, tracking, and compilation of evidence from internal teams into regulator reports
* Analyze data using a variety of software, provide recommendations based on the results, and present to internal stakeholders
* Adapt to and incorporate state and federal regulatory updates into existing policies, procedures, and assessments
* Maintain and enhance Behavioral Health (BH) network analytics models and tools to support regulatory filings and strategic analyses, ensuring data accuracy and reliability
* Conduct BH network adequacy analyses (time/distance, ratios, access gap identification) and provide insights that guide contracting, network design, and compliance decisions
* Prepare and validate BH data for regulatory submissions, including DHCS, DMHC, CMS, CDI and NCQA required reporting/filings
* Develop dashboards and deliver executive\\-level insights on BH access, capacity, utilization, claims trends, grievances, and operational stability, supporting governance committees and leadership

  
**Your Knowledge and Experience**

* Requires an MPH, MBA, MS, MA in science, social science, public health, health services research or business, or BA/BS with demonstrated equivalent work experience
* Requires 5 years of experience in Health Care (managed care, academic, or government payer)
* Intermediate to Advanced knowledge of MS Excel (able to model, use formula), Access (link tables, run queries)
* Experience with SQL/PL\\-SQL, SAS is preferred
* Good business acumen, ability to work in a team environment
* Good math, analytical and problem\\-solving skills
* Excellent communication (written and verbal) and presentation skills, previous experience in presenting to leaders
* Strong analytical and problem\\-solving skills
* Strong presentation skills
Ability to work independently, proactively, and with little direction  
* 

**Hybrid**  

  

This role requires employees to be in\\-office based on our hybrid workplace model, balancing purposeful in\\-person collaboration with flexibility. For most teams, this means coming into office two days each week. Employees living more than 50 miles from an office location will work with their manager to determine in\\-office time based on business need.


  
**ABOUT THE TEAM  

About Blue Shield of California**

As of January 2025, Blue Shield of California became a subsidiary of Ascendiun. Ascendiun is a nonprofit corporate entity that is the parent to a family of organizations including Blue Shield of California and its subsidiary, Blue Shield of California Promise Health Plan; Altais, a clinical services company; and Stellarus, a company designed to scale healthcare solutions. **Our Workplace Model**

* For most teams, this means coming into the office two days per week.

title: Sr. Sales Compensation Analyst, Ads Sales Incentive Compensation
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


The Amazon Advertising Sales Incentive Compensation team is seeking a Sr. Sales Compensation Analyst to drive analytics throughout the Ads incentive compensation program. The charter for our team is to Enable the growth of Amazon Ads through effective sales compensation design, governance and operations.  

  

The ideal candidate for the role will be an exceptionally strong analytical thinker who excels in written and verbal communication, data analysis, modeling and process improvements. They will demonstrate teamwork, relationship building and the ability to influence at all levels of the organization, while thriving in a fast\\-paced dynamic environment that has a considerable amount of ambiguity. They help deliver data\\-driven insights that will be foundational to optimizing sales planning, performance targets, and sales compensation for Amazon Ads. They will have deep experience with variable compensation plans and associated analytics to drive data driven decisions. They will partner with Amazon Ads’ sales incentive compensation design, planning, policy, and enablement teams.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

Key job responsibilities  

* Develop a deep understanding of the foundational data and key business drivers to Amazon Ads variable compensation.
* Design and implement a comprehensive reporting \\& analytical roadmap to derive key decisions. Build out capabilities for Gen AI to deliver insights into existing data, tools and workstreams. Automate repeated processes around monthly, quarterly and annual processes.
* Compensation cost controllership: Cost and administer in plan and above plan incentives (e.g., Quotas, SPIF, MBO). Support financial budgeting and forecasting processes across total portfolio.
* Strong verbal and written communications to share findings with business leaders, finance, and technical teams to influence decision\\-making and product enhancements.
* Have a passion for sales compensation and the impact the work will have on all customers.

* Automation. Build out capabilities for AI to deliver insights into existing data, tools and workstreams. Automate repeated processes around monthly, quarterly and annual processes.
* Cross Functional Collaboration. Partner with finance team to align compensation impact to financial plan. Run analysis around correlation of SIC payouts to overall financial performance.

  

About the team  

We are the innovative minds behind Amazon Advertising's sales compensation ecosystem. We are passionate about designing compensation strategies that not only reward performance, but also drive meaningful business impact.**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**

Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.
